mission.title: Install and Configure CloudNativePG (PostgreSQL Operator)
mission.description: CloudNativePG is an operator designed to manage PostgreSQL databases within Kubernetes, automating the entire lifecycle from deployment to maintenance. It integrates directly with the Kubernetes API for seamless operations, making it suitable for teams looking to adopt PostgreSQL in a cloud-native environment. Key trade-offs include reliance on Kubernetes for high availability and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the CloudNativePG Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Helm repository for CloudNativePG, allowing you to install the operator using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add cloudnative-pg https://charts.cloudnative-pg.io

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the repository where the CloudNativePG charts are hosted.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the CloudNativePG operator
steps.description:
This step installs the CloudNativePG operator in the specified namespace.

```bash

kubectl create namespace cloudnative-pg

helm install cloudnative-pg cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg --version 1.29.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the CloudNativePG operator.
> Installs the CloudNativePG operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Create a PostgreSQL cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a PostgreSQL cluster using a custom resource definition (CRD).

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: postgres.cloudnative-pg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres-cluster
  namespace: cloudnative-pg
spec:
  instances: 3
  postgresql:
    version: '15'
EOF

```

> Creates a PostgreSQL cluster named 'my-postgres-cluster' with 3 instances.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: postgres.cloudnative-pg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres-cluster
  namespace: cloudnative-pg
spec:
  instances: 3
  postgresql:
    version: '15'
EOF

steps.title: Verify the PostgreSQL cluster
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the PostgreSQL cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg

kubectl describe cluster my-postgres-cluster -n cloudnative-pg

```

> Lists the PostgreSQL clusters in the 'cloudnative-pg' namespace.
> Describes the cluster to check its status and details.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the CloudNativePG operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the CloudNativePG operator and its resources. Be aware that this will delete the PostgreSQL clusters and associated data.

```bash

helm uninstall cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg

kubectl delete namespace cloudnative-pg

```

> Uninstalls the CloudNativePG operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the CloudNativePG operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your PostgreSQL data. This step upgrades the operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg -o yaml > backup-cluster.yaml

helm upgrade cloudnative-pg cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg --version 1.29.0

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg

```

> Backs up the current cluster configuration.
> Upgrades the CloudNativePG operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the cluster is still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg -o yaml > backup-cluster.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Kubernetes cluster does not have sufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the resource limits and ensure your cluster meets the minimum requirements.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'PostgreSQL cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the PostgreSQL instances are not starting correctly due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Review the logs of the PostgreSQL pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n cloudnative-pg'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm install failed: chart not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified Helm chart version does not exist in the repository.

**Fix:**
Ensure you have added the correct Helm repository and updated it.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl apply failed: no matches for kind 'Cluster' in version 'postgres.cloudnative-pg.io/v1'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the CRD for the Cluster resource is not installed.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the CloudNativePG operator is installed correctly and that the CRDs are applied.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for PostgreSQL 15, improved backup and restore capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg' showing your PostgreSQL cluster with a status of 'Ready'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: postgres.cloudnative-pg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres-cluster
  namespace: cloudnative-pg
spec:
  instances: 3
  postgresql:
    version: '15'
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources and the necessary tools installed.
